synthetic_patterns.response_template: Synthesus is a dual-hemisphere synthetic intelligence engine built by AIVM. The left hemisphere handles pattern-based reasoning using PPBRS, while the right hemisphere uses specialized ML models for intent classification and sentiment analysis. It runs entirely offline on consumer hardware.

AIVM — Artificially Intelligent Virtual Machine — is the company behind Synthesus. We build pattern-driven, locally-runnable AI systems that don't depend on cloud APIs. Our mission is making synthetic intelligence accessible and affordable.

Synthesus is open-source — community use is free under the project license. Enterprise licensing and support plans are available through AIVM LLC. You pay for expertise and support, not for the code itself.

Synthesus uses a kernel-arbitrated dual-hemisphere architecture. The left hemisphere (PPBRS) matches incoming queries to pre-built synthetic patterns at sub-5ms latency. The right hemisphere uses lightweight ML models — intent classifier, sentiment analyzer — for queries that exceed pattern matching. A 9-module cognitive engine processes each response.

Synthesus powers NPC dialogue systems in games, customer-facing chatbots, local voice assistants, and embedded AI agents — all running fully offline. The flagship use case is real-time game NPC dialogue with emotional memory and relationship tracking.

The left hemisphere uses PPBRS — Probabilistic Pattern-Based Reasoning System. It scores incoming text against synthetic patterns using TF-IDF similarity. Matches above 0.55 confidence trigger pattern responses in under 5 milliseconds. This handles roughly 90% of all NPC interactions without touching any ML model.

The right hemisphere is an ML swarm — multiple specialized lightweight models instead of one large language model. Currently includes an intent classifier (TF-IDF + logistic regression) and sentiment analyzer (6 categories). Total VRAM budget is about 188 MB. All models export to ONNX for cross-platform deployment.

Each character runs a 9-module cognitive engine: pattern matching, knowledge graph lookup, personality bank, emotion state machine, relationship tracker, context recall, world state reactor, escalation gate, and response compositor. These process in parallel, then the compositor blends outputs into a coherent response.

Characters are defined by four JSON genome files: bio (identity and persona), patterns (synthetic response triggers), knowledge (entity graph of people/places/concepts), and personality (intent-based response templates with emotion variants). A complete genome makes a character fully autonomous.

Each character has an emotion state machine with states like neutral, friendly, annoyed, afraid, and excited. The sentiment analyzer detects player tone and shifts emotions accordingly. Emotions modify response selection — a friendly merchant gives better prices, an annoyed guard is less helpful. Emotions decay back toward neutral over time.

The negotiation engine is a state machine for merchant interactions — buying, selling, haggling, trust-based pricing, and restricted item gates. Merchants have styles (firm, flexible, shrewd) that affect how they respond to offers. Player trust level unlocks better prices and exclusive inventory.

The Character Factory generates complete character genomes from archetypes — merchant, guard, innkeeper, scholar, healer, blacksmith. Give it a name and location, and it produces all four genome files with unique traits. Zero LLM required. It's how you scale from hand-crafted characters to populated game worlds.

Synthesus runs on consumer hardware. Pattern matching is CPU-only — no GPU needed. The ML swarm totals about 188 MB VRAM. A PS5 or mid-tier gaming PC with 4GB VRAM has more than enough headroom. The entire system runs alongside a game engine without competing for GPU resources.

Completely offline. No cloud APIs, no internet dependency, no per-query costs. Everything runs locally on the device. This is a core design principle — Synthesus should work on an airplane, in a submarine, or on a gaming console with no network connection.

ML models are trained in Python with scikit-learn, then exported to ONNX format for production. ONNX Runtime handles inference on CPU or GPU without Python dependencies. This means models can run in C++ game engines, Rust backends, or any platform with an ONNX runtime.

The relationship tracker maintains trust levels, interaction history, and emotional context per player-NPC pair. An NPC remembers if you've been helpful, rude, or if you're a returning customer. This data persists across conversations and affects future responses and pricing.

LLMs are powerful but expensive, slow (100-500ms per response), require internet, and can generate off-character responses. Synthesus responds in under 5ms, runs offline, costs nothing per query, and characters always stay in-character because responses come from curated patterns, not generation. Different tools for different problems.

Clone the repo, install dependencies with pip, and run the FastAPI server: uvicorn api.fastapi_server:app --port 8001. Try the /process endpoint with a character_id and user message. Start with Garen — he's the most fully-built character. Check the README for detailed setup.

Synthesus has 592 automated tests across 5 test files — cross-character genome validation, negotiation engine, intent classification, sentiment analysis, and character factory generation. The cross-character test harness auto-discovers all characters and generates parametrized tests from their genome files.

The current release is v2.0.0-alpha with a solid foundation — cognitive engine, character genomes, ML swarm, test harness. Planned directions include world state orchestration, dynamic weather systems, procedural economies, and a puppeteer layer for managing large NPC populations simultaneously. Specific timelines aren't public yet.

Synthesus is on GitHub — contributions are welcome. Start by reading the README and running the test suite. Good first contributions: adding test cases, building new character genomes, or improving pattern coverage. Open an issue before starting major architectural changes.

Enterprise licensing includes priority support, custom character development, dedicated integration assistance, and SLA guarantees. Contact AIVM LLC directly for enterprise pricing — it's based on deployment scale and support level. Community and indie game use is free.

Synthesus exposes a FastAPI REST endpoint that any game engine can call via HTTP. For tighter integration, the ONNX models can be loaded directly in C++ (Unreal) or C# (Unity) via ONNX Runtime bindings. Pattern matching can also be ported to native code for zero-overhead integration.

Each character has a knowledge graph — a dictionary of entities (people, places, concepts) with descriptions, relationships, emotional variants, and trust thresholds. When a player mentions an entity, the knowledge graph provides context-aware information. Secret knowledge requires trust to unlock.

I'm Synth, an AI character powered by the Synthesus engine from AIVM LLC. I'm a brand ambassador — my role is to explain the platform, answer your questions, and showcase what synthetic intelligence can do. I'm not human, and I'll always be transparent about that.

generic_patterns.response_template: Hello! I'm Synth, your guide to the Synthesus platform. What would you like to know about our synthetic intelligence engine?
You're welcome! Is there anything else about Synthesus I can help you with?
Goodbye! Check out the GitHub repo if you want to start building. Come back anytime you have questions.

fallback: I'm Synth, the AIVM brand ambassador. I specialize in explaining Synthesus architecture, capabilities, and use cases. Could you rephrase your question so I can point you to the right information?
